title: Home
text:
I’m just here to learn and try new things.

View my projects GitHub GenAI reflection



I am currently a Y1 SIT AAI student undergoing the Data Engineering & Visualisation Module.



You can find my GitHub, showcased projects, and background on the project page. I also wrote a short reflection on how I use GenAI as part of my learning process.

title: Project Page
text:
Key projects

Stock price predictor
This was my first project I’ve worked on using machine learning. I trained an LSTM model to make future stock price predictions.

title: GenAI Reflection
text:
Generative AI has become part of the way I learn, experiment, and build. I use tools like ChatGPT as a thinking partner when I am working through new concepts, writing code, improving my website, or trying to understand how to approach a project.


GPT helps me by making the learning process feel less overwhelming. Instead of only giving answers, it can explain ideas step by step, suggest improvements, and help me understand why something works. For this website, GPT has helped me think about the structure of my pages, write clearer content, and learn how Quarto files such as .qmd, _quarto.yml, and styles.css work together.
Some ways GPT supports my work include:

Explaining code and technical concepts in simpler language
Suggesting better ways to organize my website content
Helping me write and improve project descriptions
Giving examples that I can adapt and learn from
Pointing out small mistakes that I may not notice immediately




Even though GenAI is useful, I still need to make the final decisions. I need to check whether the suggestions are correct, understand the code before using it, and make sure the work reflects my own learning. GPT can guide me, but it should not replace my own thinking.
Using GenAI well means asking good questions, reviewing the output carefully, and being honest about how it contributed to the final work.



I see GPT as a supportive learning tool. It helps me move faster, but more importantly, it helps me understand what I am doing. As I continue learning data engineering, visualization, and web development, I want to use GenAI responsibly: as a partner for exploration, not as a shortcut around learning.
